thesis: BlackBerry is executing a successful pivot from legacy handset brand to a pure-play cybersecurity and IoT software vendor. Q1 FY2026 marked the first profitable quarter since 2022, driven by 70% gross margins in Secure Communications and growing QNX design wins in software-defined vehicles. FY2026 guidance was raised at the lower end ($508–$538M revenue, $72–$87M adjusted EBITDA). The $10M share buyback signals management confidence. However, execution risk remains on QNX growth trajectory and the lingering legacy handset overhang.

drivers: QNX accelerates design wins in next-generation SDV and ADAS platforms, driving royalty growth above guidance.
Secure Communications successfully cross-sells Cylance AI into the UEM base, lifting ARPU and net revenue retention.
Government cybersecurity contracts provide stable, multi-year revenue streams.
Legacy patent portfolio is monetized or divested, unlocking shareholder value.
Sustained profitability and operating leverage attract institutional capital, re-rating the stock toward SaaS peers.

base_case.assumptions: Both segments execute in line with guidance; QNX grows modestly with automotive cycle recovery. Secure Communications maintains 70% gross margins and low-teens EBITDA margins. The company delivers on its FY2026 EBITDA guidance, proving the turnaround is durable. Valuation expands to 3–4x EV/Revenue, consistent with small-cap cybersecurity peers.

risks: QNX automotive adoption slows due to OEM budget cuts or competitive pressure from Linux-based alternatives.
Secure Communications faces pricing pressure from CrowdStrike, Microsoft, and Palo Alto Networks.
Legacy revenue declines faster than expected, creating a revenue gap.
Execution missteps delay profitability, forcing dilutive financing or asset sales.

catalysts.event: QNX design wins in next-generation software-defined vehicles and ADAS platforms
Secure Communications cross-sell of Cylance AI into existing UEM base
Government and defense cybersecurity contract awards
Continued progress toward sustained profitability and operating leverage
Potential divestiture or monetization of non-core patent portfolio

key_risks: QNX automotive revenue growth may underperform if OEM adoption slows.
Legacy handset-related patent revenue continues to decline.
Intense competition in endpoint security from CrowdStrike, Microsoft, and Palo Alto Networks.
Execution risk on integrating Cylance AI across the full Secure Communications stack.
business_model: Dual-platform software company: Secure Communications (Cylance) provides AI-driven endpoint security and unified endpoint management (UEM) for enterprise and government customers; QNX is a leading embedded real-time operating system for automotive, industrial, and medical IoT devices.
